questions.q: In the given reaction the major product formed is.
questions.solution: - NO 2 is not in resonance with benzene ring, does not activate the aromatic nucleophilic substitution reaction hence fluoride is not formed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The product is:
questions.solution:
The reaction is the nucleophilic aromatic substitution reaction, in which one of the substituents in the aromatic ring is replaced by a nucleophile.

Generally, aryl halides do not undergo nucleophilic substitution reaction, but here, an electron-withdrawing cyanide group is present at the para- position and fluorine is the most electronegative atom; hence, the nucleophilic displacement becomes quite rapid.

Therefore, in this reaction, the displacement of fluoride ion from 1-fluoro-4-cyanobenzene by dimethylamine occurs readily in a DMF solution on heating in the presence of copper oxide.

 

 

.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Which of the following statement is incorrect?
options: Chlorobenzene is more reactive than benzene towards electrophilic substitution reactions.

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>–</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></math>  bond in chlorobenzene is less polar than in  <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></math> .

Chlorobenzene is less reactive than  <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></math>  towards nucleophilic substitution reactions.
In chlorobenzene further electrophilic substitution takes place at ortho and para position.
questions.solution: Chlorobenzene is less reactive than benzene towards electrophilic substitution reaction due to strong  <math><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math>  effect of  <math><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></math> .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In the following sequence of reactions, the gaseous species A, B, D, E, respectively, are
questions.solution:
The first step is the chlorine release from the CFCs:

CF2Cl2+UV light→Cl·(A)+CF2Cl·(B)

The second step is the catalytic ozone destruction. In this step, one ozone molecule is destroyed.

Cl·(A)+O3 →ClO·(D)+O2

Lastly, the chlorine atom is regenerated.

ClO·(D)+O→Cl(E)+O2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

Generally, aryl halides do not undergo nucleophilic substitution reaction, but here, an electron-withdrawing nitro group is present at the ortho- and the para- position; hence, the nucleophilic displacement becomes quite rapid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Match the following.
The correct match is $\text { A } \quad B \quad C$
questions.solution:
(A) Addition of HI takes place by the formation of $1^{\circ}$ and $2^{\circ}$ carbonium ion. $2^{\circ}$ carbonium ion being more stable will form major product, i.e. 2-iodopropane.
(B) The reaction of bromine with 1-phenylethane proceed with formation of free radical. $2^{\circ}$ free radical being more stable than $1^{\circ}$ free radical, will form the product as 1-bromo-1-phenylethane.
(C) Reaction of $\mathrm{HBr}$ to an alkene follows Markownikoff rule. So, the product will be 1-bromo-1-phenylethane.
Note : Markownikoff's rule states that, electron rich component adds to carbon atom with less number of hydrogen atoms. Hence, the correct match is A-III, B-IV, C-IV. The reactions are :
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

Grignard reagent is highly reactive compound so, it is used in preparation of many important compounds. When it is reacted with water it gives alkane. In this case it will react with heavy water which will replace Mg with Deuterium.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one among the following will undergoes faster $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reaction?
questions.solution:
During substitution, when rate of reaction depends only on one concentration term (i.e. commonly a substrate) e.g- alkyl halides. The reaction is of first order and called $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reactions. Here, rate $\propto$ substrate or, rate $=k[R X]$ i.e. alkyl halide Step I $R X \stackrel{\text { Slow }}{\longrightarrow} R^{+}+X$ Step II $R^{+} \underset{\text { Fast }}{\stackrel{\mathrm{OH}^{-}}{\longrightarrow}} R \mathrm{OH}$ Such type of reaction depends upon the stability of carb-cation formed in first step. It is favoured by heavy (bulky) groups.
To show faster $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reaction and option (d) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: Which of the following alcohols will yield the corresponding alkyl chloride on reaction with concentrated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> at room temperature?
questions.solution:
The reactions of primary and secondary alcohols with HCl require the presence of a catalyst, ZnCl2.

However, with tertiary alcohols, the reaction is conducted by simply shaking with concentrated HCl at room temperature forming corresponding alkyl chloride.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: What are A and B in the following reaction?
questions.solution:
Initially, 1-bromo-3-cholorobenzene reacts with magnesium in ether. In this reaction, bromine being more reactive than chlorine selectively reacts to form Grignard reagent phenylmagnesium bromide.

Next reaction is the reaction of Grignard reagent with CH3CHO. Griganrd reagent reacts with acetaldhyde to form a secondary alcohol.

The alcohol formed is very prone to E1 elimination reaction, since the carbocation formed will be stabilised by resonance. So, the weak acid ammonium chloride is used in the work up.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The S<sub>N</sub>2 reaction involves back-side attack and therefore results in a "Walden Inversion." For which one of the substrates shown would you be able to demonstrate that such back-side attack with "Walden Inversion" has in fact occurred?
questions.solution:
For SN2 reaction:

1. It is single step reaction.

2. In this reaction nucleophile attacks from the back side of the leaving group. If chiral carbon is present then it will change its configuration.

So, to observe inversion, the substitution must occur at a chiral centre.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Chlorobenzene when heated with a mixture of concentrated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> and concentrated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>HNO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> yields _____ as a major product.
questions.solution:
It is an electrophilic substitution reaction. When chlorobenzene reacts with concentrated H2SO4 and concentrated HNO3 solution then two products are formed. One is o-nitrochlorobenzene and second is p-nitrochlorobenzene. 

p-nitrochlorobenzene is a major product.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In the nucleophilic substitution reactions <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></msub><mn>2 </mn><mi>or</mi><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">1</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> the reactivity of alkyl halides follows the sequence
questions.solution:
Nucleophilic substitution reaction depends on leaving group ability
The correct order of reactivity is
RI > RBr > RCl > RF 
It is due to fact, that weaker the base, better will be the leaving group.
Leaving group ability I − > Br − >Cl> F −
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
